name: Caribbean Guacamole
recipeIngredient: 1/2 lb Sweet bread, Hawaiian or Portugese
2 tbsp Coconut, sweetened, flaked and dried
1/4 tsp Crushed dried red hot chilies
recipeInstructions:
1. Cut the bread into 1/4-inch thick slices, then cut diagonally into triangles.
2. Arrange in a single layer in a 10x15-inch pan. Bake at 300°F until lightly browned, about 10 minutes; turn slices over halfway through baking. Cool on racks. If made ahead, wrap airtight and store at room temperature up to one day.
3. In a 7-8-inch frying pan, stir coconut over medium-high heat until golden brown, about 3 minutes. Pour from the pan; set aside.
4. Cut papaya in half lengthwise. Discard seeds and peel, leaving halves intact. From one half, cut two lengthwise slices, each about 1/4-inch thick.
5. Dice the remaining papaya; set fruit aside.
6. Cut avocado in half lengthwise. Discard pit and peel, leaving halves intact. From one half, cut two lengthwise slices, each about 1/4-inch thick.
7. Sprinkle slices with 1 teaspoon lime juice; set aside.
8. Dice the remaining avocado and mix with remaining lime juice, sugar, and red chilies.
9. On a platter, arrange avocado mixture and diced papaya side by side in separate mounds. Fan reserved avocado slices next to diced papaya, and reserved papaya slices next to avocado mixture. Sprinkle with coconut.
10. Serve with toasted bread.
